title: 第 8 節
en: ' Like one who is going to whisper she comes near to the ear ; like a woman embracing her dear friend she hums, stretched along the bow, helpful in battle.'

It is clear that if such addresses were supposed to change a bow and a bowstring into a deity or into a fetish, there would be no poet even in our days Vl] AGNI. 483 who was not an idolator or a fetish-worshipper. But suppose we were to call the bowstring, as addressed by the Vedic poet, a fetish, instead of a poetical fancy, what should we gain ?
The principal activity of Agni consists, according to Yaska, in carrying oblations to the gods or bringing the gods to the sacrifice.

There are certain gods who are praised together with Agni, viz. Indra, Soma, Varima, Par^anya, the Ritus (seasons). Vishmi and Pushan, though they share in the same offerings with Agni, were not praised together with him in the same verses '.

II. Indra. Indra belongs to the air, or the air to Indra. He and Vayu (wind) are taken by Yaska for the same being. His are the Noon-libation (madhyandinasavana), the summer, the Trish^ubh-metre, the ParU;adasa Stoma, and the Bn'hat-Saman. His companions are the Maruts, the Rudras, besides a number of deities recorded in the Nighanfti, V, 4, 5. I give them as they are found there : —
zh: 「她如同將要耳語者般靠近耳朵；如同摯友懷抱女人般低鳴，伸展於弓上，在戰鬥中助人一臂之力。」
顯然，倘若這類祈詞被認為能將弓和弓弦轉變為某位提婆或某種物靈崇拜的對象，那麼即使在我們這個時代，也不會有任何詩人不是偶像崇拜者或物靈崇拜者。但假設我們將梨俱吠陀詩人所祈禱的弓弦稱為物靈崇拜的對象，而不是詩意的虛構，我們會獲得什麼好處呢？
根據亞斯卡（Yaska）的說法，阿格尼（Agni）的主要活動在於將祭品運送給諸神，或將諸神帶往祭祀。
有某些提婆與阿格尼（Agni）一同受讚頌，即因陀羅（Indra）、蘇摩（Soma）、瓦魯那（Varima）、帕瑞尼亞（Paranya）與瑞圖（季節；Ritus）。毗濕奴（Vishnu）與普善（Pushan）雖然與阿格尼共享同樣的祭祀供品，卻未曾在同一詩節中與他一同受讚頌。

因陀羅屬於天空，或者說天空屬於因陀羅。耶斯卡（Yaska）將他與伐由（Vayu，風）視為同一個神祇。他掌司正午祭祀（madhyandinasavana）、夏季、特里什圖卜韻律（Triṣṭubh-metre）、帕魯達沙頌（Paruṣadasa Stoma）以及布里哈特薩曼讚歌（Bṛhat-Saman）。他的同伴包括馬魯特（Maruts）、魯陀羅（Rudras），以及《名義集》（Nighantu）第五卷第四、五章中記載的多位提婆。我將它們按照原文所載列舉如下：——
瓦由（風）。
跋羅阿斯跛底（Varuṇa；天空之主）。
語言、呼吸）。
部陀羅（風暴）。
阿帕姆·那帕特（Apam napat）——水之子，因陀羅（雨神）的後裔。
的水，或帕瓦尼亞（雲）(Paiv/anya)。
阿格尼（Agni）。
婆罕頻提（*Bṛhaspati*），特尤斯之子，執掌祭祀、禱告和讚頌之神。 主神雅摩（*lord Yama*）。
（演說）。
密多羅（Mitra）
婆羅門天主（*Brahmawaspati*，意為「婆羅門之主」）。
